description:
# fz-TYP: Typer perf — kill nested fixpoints, expose data-flow

## The thing in one sentence

The typer is doing standard data-flow analysis using three nested
whole-graph fixpoints where one worklist would do; the per-iteration
cost is dominated by `is_equiv` calls that lack an O(1) trivial-case
check; and the pipeline runs `type_module` an extra time for a
mutation nobody downstream reads. Fix each at the root.

## Why now

`ast_eval` compiles in ~530 ms; ~510 ms of that is the typer. A
9-line program. The slowness isn't accidental complexity — it's
structural: the current implementation is a brute-force version of
algorithms the code is already trying to be.

## Concrete acceptance

- `ast_eval` end-to-end compile drops from ~530 ms to under 50 ms.
- `type_module` call count in the codegen pipeline drops from 3 to 2.
- Within one `type_module` call, no `for _ in 0..N` whole-graph
  rebuilds remain — worklist drives termination.
- Every existing fixture passes three-path parity (interp + JIT + AOT).
- Every existing typer test passes unchanged.

## What this is NOT

- A rewrite of `Descr` operations or the set-theoretic core.
- A change to specialization policy (per-callsite keying stays).
- A change to `type_fn` (per-spec body typing stays).
- An attempt to make `Descr` operations produce canonical forms.

## Removals are first-class

Each ticket explicitly enumerates what comes out:

- TYP.2 removes the second `type_module` call from the pipeline and
  the `type_module_called_exactly_three_times` test pin.
- TYP.3 removes the outer `0..16` LFP loop, the `0..64 / 0..16` phase-2
  caps, all `specs.keys().cloned().collect()` snapshots, the
  phase-1/phase-2 split, `compute_effective_returns`'s separate
  `0..(N*4+16)` loop, and the `prev_returns` outer-iter cache.

The arc only finishes when the dead machinery is gone.

## DAG

```
TYP.1  is_equiv structural fast-path
       (one-line shortcut inside the kernel)
TYP.2  Delete redundant type_module call #2
       (rewrites are orthogonal — code comment already says so)
TYP.3  Replace nested fixpoints with one worklist
       (standard data-flow with a callee→callers reverse index)
```

No edges between the three. Each lands independently and verifiably.

## Background

See conversation 2026-05-18 for the measurement that exposed this:
- ~388 µs/type_fn call on ast_eval (vs ~65 µs on fib_tailrec) → TYP.1
- 3 type_module calls, middle one identical to first for ast_eval → TYP.2
- 11 outer fixpoint iters, 3357 walks for 58 specs → TYP.3
title: [EPIC] Typer perf: kill nested fixpoints, expose data-flow
